facts.text: Betta fish can recognize their owners and will often swim to the front of the tank when they see a familiar face.
Goldfish were first domesticated in China over 1,000 years ago during the Tang Dynasty, originally bred from wild Prussian carp.
The nitrogen cycle is the most important concept in fishkeeping — beneficial bacteria convert toxic ammonia into nitrite, then into less harmful nitrate.
Clownfish are born male and can change sex to female, with the dominant fish in a group becoming the breeding female.
Neon tetras were first discovered in the Amazon basin in 1934 and quickly became one of the most popular aquarium fish worldwide.
A properly cycled aquarium takes 4–8 weeks to establish the beneficial bacteria needed to safely support fish.
Cherry shrimp are one of the easiest invertebrates to keep and breed in freshwater aquariums, with females carrying 20–30 eggs at a time.
The Georgia Aquarium in Atlanta holds over 10 million gallons of water, making it one of the largest aquariums in the world.
Male betta fish build bubble nests on the water's surface as part of their breeding behavior, even when no female is present.
Corydoras catfish are social fish that thrive in groups of six or more and communicate through clicking sounds.
Hang-on-back (HOB) filters are among the most popular filtration systems for beginner aquariums due to their ease of use and maintenance.
Discus fish are often called the 'king of the aquarium' for their vibrant colors and elegant disc-shaped bodies.
Takashi Amano pioneered the Nature Aquarium style of aquascaping, drawing inspiration from Japanese gardens and natural landscapes.
Plecos (plecostomus) are popular algae-eating fish, but many species can grow over 12 inches long and need large tanks.
The Monterey Bay Aquarium in California is famous for its kelp forest exhibit, one of the tallest aquarium exhibits in the world.
Guppies are known as 'million fish' because of their prolific breeding — a single female can produce 20–200 fry every 30 days.
Reef aquariums require intense lighting to support photosynthetic corals, with LED fixtures becoming the modern standard.
Angelfish are cichlids native to the Amazon River basin and can grow up to 6 inches long and 8 inches tall.
The iwagumi aquascaping style uses stones as the primary hardscape element, following a rule of odd-numbered stone groupings.
Nerite snails are prized in aquariums for their appetite for algae and their inability to breed in freshwater.
Oscars are highly intelligent cichlids that can be trained to eat from their owner's hand and recognize individual people.
Canister filters provide superior mechanical, biological, and chemical filtration and are preferred for tanks over 40 gallons.
The Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium in Japan features one of the world's largest acrylic panels, measuring 27 feet tall and 74 feet wide.
Mollies are livebearers that can adapt to both freshwater and saltwater environments, making them uniquely versatile aquarium fish.
A planted tank uses live aquatic plants for filtration, oxygenation, and aesthetic appeal, creating a self-sustaining ecosystem.
Swordtail fish get their name from the elongated lower lobe of the male's tail fin, which resembles a sword.
Amano shrimp, named after aquascaper Takashi Amano, are excellent algae eaters and can consume types of algae that most fish ignore.
The pH scale measures how acidic or alkaline water is — most tropical freshwater fish prefer a pH between 6.5 and 7.5.
Mandarinfish are among the most colorful saltwater fish but are notoriously difficult to feed, often accepting only live copepods.
The Dutch aquascaping style originated in the Netherlands in the 1930s and emphasizes dense, colorful arrangements of aquatic plants.
Ghost shrimp are nearly transparent, making them fascinating to watch as you can see their internal organs working.
Zebra danios were the first fish to be genetically modified to create GloFish, which fluoresce under blue light.
Tiger barbs are notorious fin nippers and should be kept in groups of six or more to reduce aggression toward tankmates.
The world's oldest known aquarium fish was an Australian lungfish named Granddad, who lived at the Shedd Aquarium in Chicago for over 80 years.
Protein skimmers are essential equipment for saltwater and reef aquariums, removing dissolved organic compounds before they break down.
Cardinal tetras look similar to neon tetras but have a red stripe that extends the full length of their body rather than just halfway.
Biotope aquariums recreate specific natural habitats, such as an Amazon blackwater stream or an African rift lake.
Seahorses are among the only animals where the male carries and gives birth to the young, keeping eggs in a brood pouch.
Driftwood is a popular hardscape material that releases tannins into the water, creating a natural blackwater effect.
The Dubai Aquarium and Underwater Zoo in the Dubai Mall contains over 33,000 aquatic animals and one of the world's largest acrylic viewing panels.
Harlequin rasboras are peaceful schooling fish from Southeast Asia that display a distinctive triangular black patch on their body.
CO2 injection systems are used in planted tanks to boost plant growth and create lush underwater gardens.
Cichlids from Africa's Lake Malawi are known as mbuna and are famous for their brilliant colors and aggressive territorial behavior.
Sponge filters are gentle, affordable, and ideal for breeding tanks and shrimp tanks because they don't suck up fry or small invertebrates.
The S.E.A. Aquarium in Singapore (now S.E.A. Aquarium at Resorts World Sentosa) holds over 18 million gallons of water.
Mystery snails are popular aquarium pets that come in gold, blue, purple, and ivory color varieties.
Water hardness measures dissolved minerals — soft water fish like tetras and hard water fish like African cichlids have very different needs.
Gouramis have a labyrinth organ that allows them to breathe atmospheric air, letting them survive in oxygen-poor waters.
Nano tanks (under 10 gallons) have become increasingly popular for keeping shrimp, bettas, and small schooling fish on desktops.
The clown loach is a social bottom-dweller that can grow over 12 inches and live for more than 20 years in captivity.
Live rock in saltwater aquariums is made of old coral skeletons colonized by beneficial bacteria, coralline algae, and tiny organisms.
Platies are hardy livebearing fish available in dozens of color varieties, making them ideal for beginners.
Regular water changes of 10–25% weekly are one of the simplest and most effective ways to maintain a healthy aquarium.
Lionfish are strikingly beautiful but venomous saltwater fish with fan-like pectoral fins and long dorsal spines.
The kuhli loach is an eel-shaped bottom dweller from Southeast Asia that hides during the day and becomes active at night.
Aquarium test kits measure ammonia, nitrite, nitrate, and pH — the four most critical water parameters for fish health.
Goldfish can live 10–15 years on average, and some have been documented living over 40 years with proper care.
Wrasses are colorful, active saltwater fish that often bury themselves in sand at night for protection.
Substrate choices for planted tanks include nutrient-rich soil, gravel, and sand, each affecting plant growth differently.
The royal gramma is a peaceful, cave-dwelling saltwater fish known for its vivid purple and yellow coloration.
Paludariums combine aquatic and terrestrial elements, with both underwater and above-water sections in the same enclosure.
Dwarf gouramis are one of the most colorful freshwater fish, with males displaying brilliant red and blue stripes.
Bristlenose plecos are preferred over common plecos for most home aquariums because they stay under 5 inches long.
The first public aquarium opened at the London Zoo in 1853 and sparked the Victorian aquarium craze.
Yellow tang is one of the most recognizable reef aquarium fish, known for its vibrant solid yellow body.
Betta fish come in over 70 recognized color and fin varieties, including halfmoon, crowntail, and plakat types.
Aquarium heaters are essential for tropical fish, maintaining water temperatures between 76°F and 82°F (24–28°C).
Damselfish are among the hardiest saltwater fish and are often used to cycle new marine aquariums.
The Walstad method uses soil and plants to create a low-tech, self-sustaining planted aquarium with minimal equipment.
Rummy-nose tetras are excellent schooling fish whose tight formations make them a favorite for planted aquarium displays.
Butterfly fish are delicate, disc-shaped saltwater fish with intricate patterns, but most are difficult to keep in home aquariums.
Cory catfish are armored bottom dwellers that use barbels to search for food in the substrate.
The firefish goby is a peaceful, slender saltwater fish known for its elongated dorsal fin and red-to-purple gradient coloring.
Aquarium cycling can be done fishless by adding pure ammonia, which is safer for fish than traditional cycling methods.
Electric blue rams are a selectively bred color morph of the German blue ram cichlid, prized for their iridescent blue bodies.
T5 fluorescent lights were the standard for planted and reef tanks before LED technology became dominant in the 2010s.
Ember tetras are tiny (under 1 inch) orange fish that are perfect for nano planted tanks.
The flowerhorn cichlid is a man-made hybrid known for its prominent forehead hump (kok) and vibrant colors.
Gobies are one of the largest fish families, with over 2,000 species found in both freshwater and saltwater environments.
Activated carbon is used in aquarium filters to remove discoloration, odors, and dissolved chemicals from the water.
The axolotl, while not a fish, is a popular aquatic pet that retains its larval features throughout life, including external gills.
Blennies are personable, bottom-dwelling saltwater fish known for perching on rocks and peering out at their surroundings.
Java fern and Java moss are two of the easiest aquatic plants for beginners, thriving in low-light conditions.
The peacock cichlid from Lake Malawi is one of the most colorful freshwater fish, rivaling many saltwater species.
Aquarium salt is sometimes used in freshwater tanks to treat parasites and reduce stress, but it's harmful to many plants and invertebrates.
Siamese algae eaters are one of the few fish that will consume black beard algae, a common nuisance in planted tanks.
The archer fish can shoot jets of water from its mouth to knock insects off overhanging branches.
Powerheads and wavemakers create water flow in aquariums, which is essential for coral health in reef tanks.
German blue rams are small, peaceful cichlids that prefer soft, acidic water and temperatures above 80°F.
The pea puffer is the world's smallest pufferfish, growing to just 1 inch, and is popular in nano freshwater tanks.
UV sterilizers use ultraviolet light to kill free-floating algae, bacteria, and parasites in aquarium water.
Otocinclus catfish are tiny algae eaters that work in groups to keep planted tank glass and leaves clean.
Some species of coral can grow in home reef aquariums and be fragged (fragmented) to share with other hobbyists.
The ram cichlid (Mikrogeophagus ramirezi) is native to the Orinoco River basin in Venezuela and Colombia.
Fluval, Eheim, and AquaClear are among the most trusted brands in aquarium filtration equipment.
Endler's livebearers are closely related to guppies but are smaller and have their own distinct color patterns.
The nitrogen cycle bacteria Nitrosomonas and Nitrobacter are the backbone of biological filtration in aquariums.
Freshwater stingrays from South America are kept by advanced hobbyists but require very large tanks and pristine water quality.
Some aquarium fish, like the African butterflyfish, are surface dwellers that can leap out of the water to catch insects.
Airline tubing, air stones, and air pumps add supplemental oxygen and create decorative bubble effects in aquariums.
The Denison barb (roseline shark) from India became so popular in the aquarium trade that wild populations declined significantly.
Hard corals (stony corals) build calcium carbonate skeletons and are the foundation of reef ecosystems in both nature and reef tanks.
Quarantine tanks are recommended for all new fish to prevent introducing diseases into an established aquarium.
The celestial pearl danio, discovered in Myanmar in 2006, quickly became one of the most sought-after nano fish.
Aquarium glass thickness increases with tank size — a 125-gallon tank typically uses 1/2-inch glass.
Pictus catfish are active, spotted catfish with long barbels, known for their fast swimming and nocturnal behavior.
Soft corals like leather corals, mushroom corals, and zoanthids are easier to keep than hard corals for beginner reef hobbyists.
The Jack Dempsey cichlid was named after the heavyweight boxing champion due to its aggressive temperament.
Gravel vacuums (siphons) are essential tools for removing debris from substrate during water changes.
Killifish are known for their stunning colors and short lifespans — some annual species live for only one year.
The whale shark, the world's largest fish, is the star attraction of the Georgia Aquarium in Atlanta.
Feeding fish a varied diet of flakes, pellets, frozen, and live foods promotes better health and coloration.
African dwarf frogs are fully aquatic and are commonly kept alongside peaceful community fish.
The tang police is a humorous online fishkeeping term for enthusiasts who remind others that tangs need very large tanks.
Daphnia (water fleas) are a nutritious live food for aquarium fish and can be cultured at home.
Rimless aquariums have gained popularity for their clean, modern aesthetic, especially in aquascaping displays.
The betta fish (Siamese fighting fish) was historically bred for fighting in Thailand, where it is the national aquatic animal.
Reverse osmosis (RO) water is used by many fishkeepers to create pure water that can be remineralized to specific parameters.
The pearl gourami has a mosaic-like pattern of iridescent spots across its body, making it one of the most attractive gourami species.
Saltwater tangs are herbivorous fish that require plenty of algae in their diet to stay healthy and avoid lateral line disease.
Monte Carlo and dwarf baby tears are popular carpeting plants that create a lush green lawn effect on the aquarium floor.
The redtail catfish can grow up to 4 feet long, yet it is still commonly (and irresponsibly) sold in pet stores.
Aquaponics combines fish keeping with plant growing — fish waste fertilizes plants, and plants filter the water for the fish.
Threadfin rainbowfish have delicate, elongated fins and display best when kept in a well-planted tank with gentle water flow.
The blue-green chromis is one of the most peaceful damselfish and is extremely popular in saltwater community tanks.
Ich (white spot disease) is one of the most common aquarium fish diseases, caused by the parasite Ichthyophthirius multifiliis.
Acropora corals are among the most challenging but rewarding hard corals to grow in a home reef aquarium.
The black ghost knifefish generates weak electric fields to navigate and communicate in dark waters.
Fluval Spec and Fluval Flex are popular all-in-one nano aquarium kits that include built-in filtration and LED lighting.
Apistogramma are small, colorful dwarf cichlids from South America with fascinating parental behavior.
Duckweed is a fast-growing floating plant that provides shade and removes excess nutrients but can quickly take over a tank.
Blue tang (Paracanthurus hepatus) became even more popular after the release of the movie Finding Dory in 2016.
Refugiums are secondary chambers in saltwater systems that grow macroalgae and copepods to naturally improve water quality.
The weather loach (dojo loach) is said to become more active before storms due to changes in barometric pressure.
Fishless cycling using household ammonia was popularized in the early 2000s and is now the recommended method for new tanks.
Boesemani rainbowfish are stunning dual-toned fish with a blue front half and orange-yellow back half.
Mechanical filtration uses foam or filter floss to physically trap debris and particles from aquarium water.
The six-line wrasse is a colorful, hardy saltwater fish but can be territorial and bully new additions to the tank.
Anubias plants are extremely hardy, grow slowly, and can be attached to rocks and driftwood rather than planted in substrate.
Figure-eight puffers are brackish water fish that need some salt in their water and are known for their curious personalities.
Aquarium controllers like the Neptune Apex can automate temperature, lighting, dosing, and water quality monitoring.
The vampire pleco (Leporacanthicus galaxias) is a striking black fish covered in white spots.
Biological filtration relies on bacteria to convert harmful nitrogen compounds and is the most critical type of filtration.
Pygmy corydoras grow to less than 1 inch and are one of the smallest catfish species kept in aquariums.
Saltwater fish require specific gravity (salinity) between 1.020 and 1.026 measured with a refractometer or hydrometer.
The paradise fish was one of the first tropical fish imported to Europe in the 1860s, sparking interest in the hobby.
Bamboo shrimp are filter feeders that position themselves in the current and use fan-like hands to catch microscopic food particles.
Red-bellied piranhas are actually quite shy in aquariums and rarely display the frenzied feeding seen in nature documentaries.
Hornwort is a fast-growing stem plant that absorbs excess nutrients and helps prevent algae growth in aquariums.
The watchman goby forms a symbiotic relationship with pistol shrimp — the shrimp digs a burrow and the goby stands guard.
API Master Test Kit is one of the most popular liquid test kits used by hobbyists to check ammonia, nitrite, nitrate, and pH.
Tidal pools served as some of nature's first aquariums and inspired early aquarists to keep marine life at home.
Glass catfish are completely transparent, allowing you to see their skeleton and internal organs.
The Betta imbellis (peaceful betta) is less aggressive than the common Betta splendens and can sometimes be kept in pairs.
Aquascaping competitions, such as the IAPLC (International Aquatic Plants Layout Contest), draw thousands of entries from around the world.
Green spotted puffers are popular brackish water fish that need to transition to full saltwater as adults.
Cryptocoryne plants are versatile, low-light species that come in many varieties and are staples in planted tanks.
The wolf cichlid (Dovii) from Central America is one of the most aggressive freshwater fish kept in aquariums.
Sand-sifting gobies in reef tanks swallow mouthfuls of sand and filter out tiny organisms and detritus.
Hornwort can grow up to 6 inches per week under good conditions, making it a powerful nutrient absorber.
The blue discus is a wild color form from the Amazon that has been selectively bred into many stunning domestic varieties.
Auto top-off (ATO) systems automatically replace water that evaporates from aquariums, maintaining stable salinity in saltwater tanks.
Chili rasboras are one of the smallest aquarium fish at under 0.8 inches, perfect for nano tanks.
Fin rot is a bacterial infection made worse by poor water quality and stress, causing fins to fray and disintegrate.
The flowerhorn cichlid is considered a symbol of good luck and prosperity in many Asian cultures.
Scarlet badis are micro predators from India that are among the smallest known freshwater fish, reaching only 0.8 inches.
Biological media like ceramic rings and bio-balls provide surface area for beneficial bacteria colonies in aquarium filters.
The magnificent foxface rabbitfish has venomous dorsal spines but is generally peaceful and an excellent algae eater.
Aquarium backgrounds, whether painted, film, or 3D, can dramatically change the appearance and perceived depth of a tank.
Guppy grass (Najas guadalupensis) is an undemanding floating plant that provides excellent cover for fry.
The mandarin goby's vivid blue-green and orange patterns are produced by cellular pigment rather than structural color, making it unique among fish.
Hillstream loaches are adapted to fast-flowing streams and have a flattened body that helps them cling to rocks.
Seachem Prime is one of the most popular water conditioners, neutralizing chlorine, chloramine, ammonia, and heavy metals.
The convict cichlid is one of the easiest cichlids to breed in captivity, often reproducing in community tanks.
Pothos plants can be grown with their roots submerged in aquarium water, helping absorb nitrates.
Fluval Stratum and ADA Aqua Soil are popular planted tank substrates that buffer pH and provide nutrients for root-feeding plants.
Cleaner wrasses set up 'cleaning stations' on reefs where larger fish line up to have parasites removed from their bodies.
The red arowana from Southeast Asia is one of the most expensive aquarium fish, with some specimens selling for over $300,000.
Bubble tip anemones are the most commonly kept anemone in home reef tanks and are natural hosts for clownfish.
Ember tetras, chili rasboras, and pygmy corydoras are the 'holy trinity' of nano tank stocking.
The Malawi eye-biter is a rare predatory cichlid that reportedly attacks the eyes of other fish.
LED aquarium lights can be programmed for sunrise and sunset simulations, creating a natural light cycle for fish.
The mudskipper is a fish that can walk on land, climb trees, and breathe air through its skin.
Indian almond leaves (Catappa leaves) release tannins that lower pH, add beneficial compounds, and mimic natural blackwater conditions.
The green chromis is the most popular schooling fish in marine aquariums due to its hardiness and peaceful nature.
Marimo moss balls are actually a form of algae (Aegagropila linnaei) rolled into balls by lake currents.
Panda corydoras have a distinctive black-and-white pattern resembling a panda bear and stay under 2 inches.
UV sterilizers work by exposing water to ultraviolet light as it passes through a chamber, killing pathogens without adding chemicals.
The four-eyed fish (Anableps) can see above and below the water surface simultaneously thanks to divided pupils.
Tiger shrimp (Caridina cantonensis) come in many selectively bred grades, with higher grades commanding premium prices.
The butterfly telescope goldfish has protruding eyes and flowing fins, bred in China for its ornamental appearance.
Seachem, API, and Fluval are three of the most recognized brands in the aquarium hobby worldwide.
The cherry barb from Sri Lanka is a peaceful, schooling fish that males turn vibrant red when in breeding condition.
HOB (hang-on-back) filters are popular for tanks up to 55 gallons because they're easy to install and maintain.
Pufferfish can inflate their bodies by swallowing water or air to appear larger and deter predators.
Walter worms, microworms, and baby brine shrimp are popular first foods for newly hatched fish fry.
The banggai cardinalfish from Indonesia is one of the few marine fish successfully bred in captivity by hobbyists.
Monte Carlo (Micranthemum tweediei) is a fast-spreading carpeting plant popular in aquascaping competitions.
The Florida gar is a prehistoric-looking fish with a long snout and armored scales that can breathe air.
Copepods and rotifers are tiny crustaceans cultivated as live food for reef aquarium fish and coral.
The Walking catfish can survive out of water for hours and 'walk' across land using its pectoral fins.
Drip acclimation slowly adjusts new fish to your tank's water parameters by dripping tank water into the bag over 30–60 minutes.
The peacock mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a .22 caliber bullet and has been known to break aquarium glass.
Rotala rotundifolia is a common stem plant that turns red under high light and is easy to propagate by trimming and replanting.
The electric yellow lab (Labidochromis caeruleus) is one of the most peaceful and colorful Lake Malawi cichlids.
Aquarium sumps are secondary tanks placed below the display tank that house filtration equipment and increase total water volume.
Some goldfish varieties, like the ranchu and lionhead, have been bred for centuries to have no dorsal fin.
The longfin clownfish is a captive-bred variant with flowing, elongated fins not found in wild clownfish.
Water sprite (Ceratopteris thalictroides) can be planted in substrate or left floating and grows rapidly under moderate light.
The frontosa cichlid from Lake Tanganyika is a slow-growing, regal fish with distinctive vertical black bars and a nuchal hump.
Titanium heaters are virtually unbreakable and preferred for aggressive fish that might damage glass heaters.
The Indian dwarf puffer is entirely freshwater and is one of the smallest vertebrates on the planet.
Adult clownfish can change sex — if the dominant female dies, the largest male will become female.
Bucephalandra is a slow-growing epiphyte plant from Borneo that attaches to rocks and has iridescent, jewel-like leaves.
The powder blue tang is one of the most visually striking saltwater fish but is prone to ich in home aquariums.
ADA (Aqua Design Amano) is a premium aquascaping brand founded by Takashi Amano in 1982 in Japan.
Sterbai corydoras are one of the few cory species that tolerate the higher temperatures preferred by discus fish.
Refugium macro algae like chaetomorpha absorb nitrates and phosphates, acting as natural filtration in reef systems.
Archerfish can adjust for the refraction of light at the water surface when aiming their water jets at prey above.
Snowflake clownfish are a designer variety bred from ocellaris clownfish with unique white pattern mutations.
Black beard algae (BBA) is one of the most stubborn algae in planted tanks, often caused by inconsistent CO2 levels.
Galaxy rasboras (celestial pearl danios) have dark blue bodies covered in pearl-like white spots and red fins.
The sailfin pleco features a large, sail-like dorsal fin and can grow over 18 inches in captivity.
Melafix and Pimafix are popular over-the-counter antibacterial treatments derived from tea tree and West Indian bay oils.
Parrotfish produce a mucus cocoon to sleep in at night, which protects them from parasites and predators.
Spawning mops made from acrylic yarn are used by killifish breeders to collect eggs for incubation.
The leopard wrasse is known for burying itself completely in sand and is challenging to acclimate to aquarium life.
Red root floaters (Phyllanthus fluitans) develop deep red undersides under strong lighting and are popular in nano tanks.
The Oscar fish can rearrange tank decorations by moving gravel and pushing objects around with its mouth.
Calcium reactors maintain calcium and alkalinity levels in reef tanks by dissolving aragonite media with CO2.
The oldest goldfish ever recorded, named Tish, lived to be 43 years old in the United Kingdom.
Snowflake moray eels are popular in saltwater aquariums because they tolerate other fish better than most moray species.
Root tabs are fertilizer capsules pushed into aquarium substrate to feed heavy root-feeding plants like Amazon swords.
The redtail black shark is not a true shark but a cyprinid fish from Thailand with a jet-black body and bright red tail.
Green star polyps (GSP) are one of the easiest and fastest-growing corals for beginner reef hobbyists.
The concept of 'one inch of fish per gallon' is a widely cited but oversimplified rule that doesn't account for fish body mass or bioload.
Cuttlefish can change the color and texture of their skin in less than a second, displaying some of the fastest camouflage in the animal kingdom.
Bristlenose plecos breed readily in cave-like structures, with males guarding the eggs until they hatch.
The chocolate gourami is a beautiful but delicate fish from peat swamps that requires very soft, acidic water.
Aquarium co-ops and local fish clubs are community groups where hobbyists trade fish, plants, and equipment.
The Siamese flying fox is often confused with the Siamese algae eater but is a less effective algae consumer.
Aquarium dosing pumps automate the addition of fertilizers, trace elements, and supplements on a precise schedule.
Hagfish can produce gallons of slime in seconds as a defense mechanism, clogging the gills of predators.
Super red cherry shrimp are selectively bred to have solid, deep red coloration throughout their entire bodies.
The synodontis catfish from Africa includes species that swim upside down, earning them the name 'upside-down catfish.'
Amazon sword plants are large, easy-to-grow rosette plants that serve as stunning centerpiece plants in freshwater tanks.
The blue eye rainbowfish from Australia is a small, active fish with brilliant blue eyes that shimmer under light.
Quarantine protocols typically involve keeping new fish isolated for 2–4 weeks while observing for signs of disease.
Tridacna clams in reef aquariums use photosynthetic algae in their mantles to produce food from light.
The keyhole cichlid is one of the most peaceful South American cichlids, making it suitable for community tanks.
Eheim classic canister filters have been in production since the 1960s and are known for their reliability and quiet operation.
The threadfin acara is an underappreciated cichlid with an iridescent blue-green body and delicate fin extensions.
Malaysian trumpet snails burrow through substrate, preventing gas pockets and aerating the soil in planted tanks.
Aquarium-safe silicone is used to seal glass tanks and is free of anti-fungal additives that could harm fish.
Lungfish can survive for years in a state of estivation, burrowed in dried mud and breathing air until rains return.
The sunset platy is a selectively bred variation with a gradient from yellow-orange to deep red across its body.
Lava rock is an affordable, porous hardscape material that provides surface area for beneficial bacteria in aquariums.
Marine ich (Cryptocaryon irritans) is different from freshwater ich and requires different treatment approaches.
The starlight bristlenose pleco has tiny white spots on a dark body, resembling a star-filled night sky.
Aquarium CO2 can be generated with DIY yeast-and-sugar setups or precision pressurized systems with solenoid valves.
Wobbegong sharks, also known as carpet sharks, are ambush predators that blend seamlessly with rocky reef bottoms.
The GBR (German blue ram) is extremely sensitive to water quality and often considered a good indicator of tank health.
Vallisneria (jungle val) is a fast-growing background plant that spreads via runners and creates a grass-like curtain effect.
Royal dottyback is a small, vibrant purple and yellow fish that can be aggressive despite its modest size.
The rule of thumb for stocking cichlid tanks is to overstock slightly to spread aggression among many fish.
Water wisteria (Hygrophila difformis) is a fast-growing stem plant that can also be floated to provide shade.
The ocean sunfish (Mola mola) is the heaviest known bony fish, weighing up to 5,000 pounds.
Fluval FX6 is one of the most powerful canister filters available, rated for aquariums up to 400 gallons.
The ember tetra's warm orange color intensifies in tanks with dark substrate and plenty of live plants.
Copperband butterflyfish are often purchased to eat aiptasia anemones in reef tanks, though not all individuals will do so.
Seachem Flourish is a popular comprehensive liquid fertilizer for planted freshwater aquariums.
The spotted raphael catfish produces a croaking sound when removed from water, using its pectoral fin spines.
Electric eels are not true eels but knifefish that can generate up to 860 volts of electricity.
Staurogyne repens is a compact foreground plant that stays low and bushy, ideal for the front of planted tanks.
The flame angelfish is a vibrant red-orange dwarf angel that is one of the most popular small saltwater fish.
Assassin snails are carnivorous freshwater snails used to control pest snail populations in aquariums.
Fish tuberculosis (Mycobacterium marinum) can be transmitted to humans through open cuts, causing skin infections.
The Congo tetra is a larger tetra species known for its flowing fins and iridescent rainbow body coloring.
Some deep-sea fish produce their own light through bioluminescence, using symbiotic bacteria or chemical reactions.
Hygger and Nicrew are budget-friendly LED light brands popular among planted tank hobbyists.
The pearl scale goldfish has raised, dome-like scales that give it a golf ball-like appearance.
Repashy gel foods are made by mixing powder with boiling water and letting it set, providing balanced nutrition for many fish species.
Blue velvet shrimp are a color morph of Neocaridina davidi, selectively bred for deep blue coloration.
Tank-bred marine fish are hardier than wild-caught specimens and help reduce pressure on wild populations.
Cleaner shrimp like the scarlet skunk cleaner shrimp set up cleaning stations in reef tanks and will clean parasites from fish.
The kribensis cichlid is a small, colorful West African fish known for breeding in caves and plant pots.
Purigen by Seachem is a synthetic filtration media that removes organic waste and discoloration from aquarium water.
Medaka (Japanese rice fish) have been kept as pets in Japan for centuries and were among the first fish sent to space.
The spotted puffer can crunch through hard-shelled invertebrates with its beak-like teeth and needs snails in its diet to keep teeth worn.
Spider wood (also called azalea root) is a lightweight, branchy driftwood popular for intricate aquascaping layouts.
Whale sharks filter up to 6,000 liters of water per hour — they're gentle giants that eat only plankton and small fish.
The albino bristlenose pleco is a tank-bred color morph with a pale yellow body and red eyes.
AquaSoil by ADA actively lowers pH and softens water, making it ideal for South American biotope setups.
The foxface rabbitfish changes color when stressed or sleeping, turning from bright yellow to a mottled brown.
Lily pipes are glass inlet and outlet devices used in planted tanks for a clean, minimalist aesthetic.
Melanotaenia boesemani, the Boeseman's rainbowfish, is endemic to a few lakes in the Vogelkop Peninsula of New Guinea.
The barreleye fish has a transparent head that allows it to look upward through its skull to spot prey silhouettes above.
Inline heaters are installed on canister filter tubing and keep equipment out of the display tank for a cleaner look.
The vampire tetra (Hydrolycus scomberoides) has enormous fangs and is one of the most fearsome-looking freshwater fish.
Fluval Bug Bites are insect-based fish food formulated to mimic the natural diet of many freshwater species.
Nassarius snails are popular in saltwater tanks as a cleanup crew, quickly emerging from sand when food is detected.
The ancient Romans kept moray eels in ornamental ponds, decorating them with jewelry and hand-feeding them.
Clownfish have a mucus coating that protects them from the stinging tentacles of their host anemone.
Tiger lotus (Nymphaea zenkeri) grows dramatic red and green lily pads and adds striking color to planted aquariums.
The engineer goby lives in burrows and constantly rearranges sand and rubble, dramatically altering the tank landscape.
Flourish Excel by Seachem is a liquid carbon supplement used as an alternative to CO2 injection in lower-tech planted tanks.
The regal angelfish is considered one of the most beautiful saltwater angelfish but is notoriously difficult to keep.
Fish can experience stress from sudden changes in lighting, which is why gradual sunrise/sunset lighting schedules are beneficial.
Some blind cave fish (Astyanax mexicanus) lost their eyes over evolutionary time because they provide no advantage in total darkness.
The candy cane coral (Caulastrea furcata) is a hardy LPS coral suitable for beginning reef aquarists.
Manzanita wood is a durable, non-tannin-releasing hardscape option popular in both freshwater and reef aquascapes.
Tropheus cichlids from Lake Tanganyika are herbivorous, colorful, and must be fed a high-fiber, low-protein diet.
Aquarium glue (cyanoacrylate gel) is fish-safe and commonly used to attach plants and corals to hardscape materials.
The Endlicheri bichir is an ancient, dinosaur-like fish that breathes air and can survive brief periods out of water.
Flashlight fish have bioluminescent organs beneath their eyes that they use to communicate, find prey, and confuse predators.
The Christmas wrasse gets its name from its festive green and red coloration.
Aquarium hobbyists often name the 'new tank syndrome' — the spike in ammonia and nitrite that occurs in uncycled aquariums.
Windelov Java fern is a variant with finely branched leaf tips that creates a delicate, lacy appearance.
The fairy wrasse genus Cirrhilabrus contains dozens of brilliantly colored species popular in reef aquariums.
Calcium, alkalinity, and magnesium are the three key parameters reef keepers must maintain for healthy coral growth.
Several species of cavefish have independently evolved to lose their eyes and pigmentation across different continents.
The freshwater pom pom crab has small anemones attached to its claws that it waves around to capture food particles.
Feeding rings keep floating food in one area, preventing it from getting sucked into the filter.
Tropica and Anubias are well-known brands that supply tissue-cultured aquarium plants free of pests and algae.
The lemon tetra has translucent yellow fins and ruby-red eyes, looking best against a dark background.
Egg crate (light diffuser panels) is placed under live rock in saltfish tanks to distribute weight and prevent cracking.
Piranhas are actually omnivorous and eat fruits, seeds, and plants in addition to meat.
The yellow watchman goby is a popular beginner saltwater fish known for its symbiosis with pistol shrimp.
Frogbit (Limnobium laevigatum) is a floating plant with round leaves and long trailing roots that provides shade for fish.
The flagfish (Jordanella floridae) is a native North American fish that eats hair algae and is named for its pattern resembling the American flag.
Oase, Fluval, and Eheim are top brands for canister filters, each with loyal followings in the hobby.
Freshwater moray eels exist — species like Gymnothorax tile can adapt to fully freshwater conditions.
The ancient Sumerians are believed to have kept fish in artificial ponds around 4,500 years ago, making them among the earliest fishkeepers.
Drop checkers filled with pH reagent provide a real-time visual indication of CO2 levels in planted aquariums.
The sailfin tang (Zebrasoma veliferum) unfurls its large dorsal and anal fins to appear larger to predators.
Fluval Plant 3.0 LED is a popular full-spectrum light with app-controlled scheduling for planted tanks.
The marble hatchetfish has a deep, keel-shaped body and can leap from the water by rapidly beating its pectoral fins.
Coral banding (fragging) allows hobbyists to propagate corals, share them, and even sell them commercially.
Seahorses are poor swimmers and rely on their prehensile tails to anchor themselves to seagrass and coral.
The bristlemouth fish is believed to be the most abundant vertebrate on Earth, numbering in the trillions in the deep ocean.
Aquarium fish shows and auctions are held by local clubs where hobbyists exhibit rare fish and bid on unusual species.
The emerald crab is used in reef tanks to control bubble algae (Valonia), one of the hobby's most annoying pests.
Dry start method (DSM) involves growing carpeting plants emerged before flooding the tank, ensuring better root establishment.
Rope fish (reedfish) are eel-shaped, air-breathing fish from West Africa that are escape artists and need tight-fitting tank lids.
Goldfish were originally silver — the orange coloration is a mutation that Chinese breeders selectively bred over a thousand years.
National Aquarium Day celebrates public aquariums and the importance of marine conservation.
World Oceans Day raises awareness about the ocean's role in our lives and the importance of protecting marine ecosystems.
The Monterey Bay Aquarium opened on October 20, 1984 and transformed the former Hovden Cannery into a world-class marine exhibit.
Finding Nemo was released on May 30, 2003 and caused a massive surge in demand for clownfish and blue tang.
The London Zoo's Fish House, the world's first public aquarium, opened on May 10, 1853.
National Goldfish Day celebrates one of the most popular and historically significant pet fish.
World Fish Migration Day raises awareness about the importance of free-flowing rivers for migratory fish.
The Georgia Aquarium opened on November 23, 2005 with the largest collection of aquatic animals in the world at the time.
Finding Dory was released on June 17, 2016, increasing awareness of blue tang conservation needs.
Takashi Amano, the father of modern aquascaping, was born on July 18, 1954 in Niigata, Japan.
Takashi Amano passed away on August 4, 2015, leaving behind a legacy that transformed aquarium keeping into an art form.
The Shedd Aquarium in Chicago opened on May 30, 1930 and was the first inland aquarium with a permanent saltwater collection.
The Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium opened on November 1, 2002 and features the massive Kuroshio Sea exhibit.
The first tropical fish were imported to Europe in the 1860s, sparking the birth of the modern aquarium hobby.
National Aquarium in Baltimore opened on August 8, 1981 and helped revitalize the city's Inner Harbor.
The Dubai Aquarium and Underwater Zoo opened in November 2008 inside the Dubai Mall.
International Year of the Reef was first declared in 1997 to raise awareness about coral reef conservation.
The GloFish, the first genetically modified pet, was introduced to the US market in December 2003.
World Reef Awareness Day highlights the importance of protecting coral reef ecosystems worldwide.
The International Aquatic Plants Layout Contest (IAPLC) was founded by Takashi Amano in 2001.
National Pet Fish Day is believed to have started by aquarium hobbyists to celebrate the joy of keeping pet fish.
The neon tetra disease (Pleistophora hyphessobryconis) causes loss of color, cysts, and is unfortunately incurable once contracted.
Freshwater angelfish can lay 100–1,000 eggs on a flat surface and both parents take turns guarding them.
Zoanthids come in hundreds of named color morphs with creative names like 'Utter Chaos,' 'Eagle Eye,' and 'Fruit Loop.'
The dwarf seahorse (Hippocampus zosterae) is only about 1 inch tall and can be kept in nano marine tanks with daily live food feedings.
Aquarium fish can suffer from swim bladder disorder, causing them to float uncontrollably or sink to the bottom.
The African cichlid hobby is so popular that Lake Malawi, Tanganyika, and Victoria each have dedicated online communities.
Clown pleco (Panaqolus maccus) stays under 4 inches and is a wood-eating pleco that requires driftwood in its diet.
Protein skimmers work by creating fine bubbles that attract organic waste, which is then collected in a cup for removal.
The African butterfly fish (Pantodon buchholzi) is a surface predator that can glide short distances above the water.
Riccia fluitans is a floating liverwort that Takashi Amano famously tied to mesh to create underwater carpets.
The pajama cardinalfish has a unique pattern with a yellow head, dark band at the middle, and spotted rear body.
Turbo snails are voracious algae grazers in saltwater tanks and can clean a glass panel in hours.
The upside-down jellyfish (Cassiopea) rests on the seafloor with its tentacles facing up to photosynthesize through symbiotic algae.
Tap water conditioners like Seachem Prime and API Stress Coat are essential because chlorine and chloramine are lethal to fish.
The frogfish can swallow prey almost its own size by rapidly expanding its mouth in as little as 6 milliseconds.
Lemon tetras develop more vivid yellow coloring when kept in a large school in a well-planted aquarium.
Bubble coral (Plerogyra sinuosa) inflates grape-like vesicles during the day that retract at night to reveal sweeper tentacles.
The three-spot gourami is named for three spots — two on its body and one represented by its eye.
Aquarium backgrounds help fish feel more secure by eliminating the see-through back wall, reducing stress and improving color.
Vampire crabs (Geosesarma) are colorful semi-terrestrial crabs popular in paludariums, needing both land and water areas.
Hammer coral (Euphyllia ancora) has flowing tentacles with hammer-shaped tips and is a popular beginner LPS coral.
Threadfin gobies use their elongated dorsal fin rays to communicate and signal to potential mates.
The world's smallest fish, Paedocypris progenetica, measures only 7.9mm and was discovered in Sumatran peat swamps in 2006.
